---
title: "Maryland_Bridges"
output: html_document
date: "2026-06-22"
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
Maryland_bridges<-read.csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/refs/heads/main/data/2018/2018-11-27/baltimore_bridges.csv")

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  view()
#Checking if average tarffic is cleaned column of vehicles ?
Maryland_bridges%>%
  select(avg_daily_traffic,vehicles)

Maryland_bridges<-Maryland_bridges %>%
  mutate(inspection_yr=inspection_yr+2000)


```
Seems to
```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
select(-vehicles)%>%
count(lat,long,avg_daily_traffic,sort=TRUE)
```
Data seems to be duplicated 
```{r}
#Condition of Bridges
Maryland_bridges<-Maryland_bridges %>%
  mutate(decade=10*(yr_built %/% 10))
  count(bridge_condition,sort=TRUE)

#How does condition of a bridge depend upon date of construction ?
Maryland_bridges%>%
  count(yr_built,sort=TRUE)%>%
  ggplot(aes(yr_built,n))+
  geom_line()

Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(yr_built>= 1900)%>%
  mutate(decade=10*(yr_built %/% 10))%>%
  count(decade,sort=TRUE)%>%
  ggplot(aes(decade,n))+
  geom_line()+
  expand_limits(y=0)+
  labs(y="Number of bridges built")
  
```
How do condition of bridges built depend the decade? 

```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(bridge_condition== "Poor")
  count(Inspection_yr,sort=TRUE)
```
  
  
```{r}
library(scales)
Maryland_bridges <- Maryland_bridges%>%
  mutate(decade=10*(yr_built %/% 10))

Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(yr_built >= 1900)%>%
  group_by(decade)%>%
  summarise(pct_good=mean(bridge_condition=="Good"),
                          total=n())%>%
  ggplot(aes(decade,pct_good))+
  geom_line()+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=percent_format())+
  expand_limits(y=0)

```

Whose is reposibel fo rteh mainatainanc eo fteh brdges ?
```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(owner!= responsibility) 

Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(!is.na(responsibility))%>%
  count(responsibility,sort=TRUE)%>%
  mutate(responsibility=reorder(responsibility,n))%>%
  ggplot(aes(responsibility,n))+
  geom_col()+
  coord_flip()

  
  
  



```
How does percent of good brdges vary acoss time acroos different stakeholders ?
```{r}

Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(yr_built >= 1900)%>%
  group_by(responsibility =  fct_lump(responsibility,4),decade)%>%
  summarise(pct_good=mean(bridge_condition=="Good"),
                          total=n())%>%
  filter(responsibility != "Other")%>%
  ggplot(aes(decade,pct_good,color=responsibility))+
  geom_line()+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=percent_format())+
  expand_limits(y=0)+
  labs(y="% of Bridges rated 'Good")
```
```{r}
#County highway authority had teh best bridges till 1970,after that every agency caught up except for Sattate tol authority

Maryland_bridges%>%
  ggplot(aes(avg_daily_traffic))+
  geom_histogram()+
  scale_x_log10(labels=comma_format())
```
```{r}
#How does vehicl etraffic affcet bridge condition ?

Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(yr_built>=1990)%>%
  group_by(traffic=cut(avg_daily_traffic, c(0,1000,10000,Inf),labels("<1000",
                                                                       "1000-10000",
                                                                       ">10000+")))%>%
  summarize(pct_good=mean(condition=="Good"),total=n())
```


```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  mutate(responsibility=fct_lump(responsibility,4))%>%
  ggplot(aes(long,lat,color=responsibility))+
  annotation_borders("state",regions="maryland")+
  coord_map()+
  geom_point(size=1)+
  theme_void()
```
```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  ggplot(aes(long,lat,color=bridge_condition))+
  annotation_borders("state",regions="maryland")+
  coord_map()+
  geom_point(size=1)+
  theme_void()
```
```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  ggplot(aes(long,lat,color=yr_built))+
  annotation_borders("state",regions="maryland")+
  coord_map()+
  geom_point(size=1)+
  theme_void()
```
```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  ggplot(aes(long,lat,color=county))+
  annotation_borders("state",regions="maryland")+
  coord_map()+
  geom_point(size=1)+
  theme_void()
```
```{r}
Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(yr_built>=1900)%>%
  group_by(decade=10*(yr_built %/% 10),county)%>%
  summarize(pct_good=mean(bridge_condition =="Good"),total=n())%>%
  ggplot(aes(decade,pct_good,color=county))+
  geom_line()+
  scale_y_continuous(labels=label_percent())+
  expand_limits(y=0)
```
##Effet of county resposiblity  on Bridge Condition 
```{r}
#OLS model
Maryland_bridges%>%
  mutate(good=bridge_condition=="Good")%>%
  glm(good~yr_built,data=.)%>%
  summary()

#Logit Model
library(broom)
model<-Maryland_bridges%>%
  mutate(good=bridge_condition=="Good")%>%
  glm(good~yr_built,data=.,family="binomial")

augment(model,Maryland_bridges,type.predict="response")%>%
  ggplot(aes(yr_built,.fitted))+
  geom_line()

```
```{r}
#Spline model
bridges <-Maryland_bridges%>%
  filter(yr_built>=1900)
  

library(splines)
model_S<-Maryland_bridges_filtered%>%
  mutate(good=bridge_condition=="Good")%>%
  glm(good~ns(yr_built,2),data=.,family="binomial")

augment(model_S,,Maryland_bridges_filtered,type.predict="response")%>%
  ggplot(aes(yr_built,.fitted))+
  geom_line()
```
```{r}
  model_T <- bridges %>%
  mutate(good = bridge_condition == "Good") %>%
  glm(good ~ ns(yr_built, 4) + responsibility + county, data = ., family = "binomial",na.action=na.exclude)

augment(model_T, bridges, type.predict = "response") %>%
  ggplot(aes(yr_built, .fitted,color=responsibility)) +
  geom_line() +
  expand_limits(y = 0) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(y = "Predicted probability a bridge is rated 'Good'")
```

```
